title: Trading Partner Agreements Are Not Required By Hipaa
source_filename: Trading Partner Agreements Are Not Required By Hipaa.docx

qas.q: In order to send accurate standard transmissions, everyone in a covered entity must be trained to know the standard transmission (EDI) format.
If the patient's copayment is more than the total amount of services rendered during an office visit, what amount is to be collected from the patient?
qas.a: Collect only the amount of the services

qas.q: Which of the following can be confirmed with an online eligibility report?
Which of the following would NOT be an advantage of using EDI
qas.a: To avoid all errors in submission of claims

qas.q: What is the main purpose of standardized transactions and code sets under HIPAA?
qas.a: To provide a common standard for the transfer of healthcare information
Standards for describing diseases, symptoms, injuries, and actions
Five-place codes using all numbers - and sometimes a combination of four numbers and one letter
Standard transactions to streamline major health insurance processes

qas.q: Trading partner agreements are important because they
qas.a: Restrict the partners from setting up their own arrangements for electronic claims
Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)

qas.q: Implementation guides for the standard transactions may be obtained from the
If the HIPAA officer finds that the trading partner has changed the formatting of a standard transaction, they may report the partner to
qas.a: The Office of E-Health Standards and Services

qas.q: Trading partner agreements are only for electronic standard transactions
